claims.claim: stt.py:62 shows the Ink-2 wire protocol with (turn.eager_end -> turn.resume?)* loop
EagerEndOfTurn = medium confidence turn ending, begin speculative reasoning
claims.found: EagerEndOfTurn: medium confidence turn ending, begin speculative reasoning (lines 543-544)

claims.claim: TurnResumed = user continues, cancel speculative work
claims.found: TurnResumed: user continues, cancel speculative work (line 545)
EndOfTurn: high confidence completion, finalize response (line 547)

claims.claim: Speculative reasoning rarely diverges from the final text
claims.found: Flux is designed to keep transcripts highly consistent between eager and final turn boundaries. Speculative reasoning rarely diverges from the final text. (lines 549-551)

claims.claim: eager_eot_threshold and eot_threshold are two separate confidence tiers
claims.found: Thresholds such as eager_eot_threshold and eot_threshold allow teams to tune the speed-stability trade-off (line 548)

claims.claim: Vapi greedy inference: immediately send utterance to LLM, cancel/restart if wrong, user never hears scrapped attempt
claims.found: Problem #5: Coordination -- Greedy Inference: when we think the user is done, immediately send their utterance to the LLM... instantly cancel and restart with the complete updated utterance. The user never hears the scrapped attempt. (lines 44-47)
